name: Montserrat Millennium Trail
name_original: Roca Foradada, La Portella – Les Agulles – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the difficult Roca Foradada, La Portella – Les Agulles – Circular Route, a 3.9-mile trail with 1626 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Marganell offers access to the Montserrat Natural Park, characterized by its distinctive "sawed" mountain range. The landscape features rounded, steep peaks and deep gorges, formed by the intensive weathering of conglomerate rock. This geological formation creates a dramatic backdrop for outdoor pursuits, including impressive cliffs and unique rock formations. The region provides a diverse range of trails catering to various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Marganell The most popular hiking route is Montserrat Millennium Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route is one of the recommended walks around Marganell. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sant Miquel Hermitage loop from Monistrol de Montserrat, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to the historic Sant Miquel Hermitage. Local hikers also love the Sant Jeroni Summit – Sant Jeroni Summit loop from Monistrol de Montserrat, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail leading through the Montserrat mountain range, often completed in about 9 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Marganell is defined by the Montserrat mountain range's steep peaks, deep gorges, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging treks. The routes in Marganell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Marganell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Marganell?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails documented around Marganell. These routes vary in difficulty, with 1 easy, 6 moderate, and 13 difficult options, primarily within the Montserrat Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Marganell?
A: The terrain around Marganell is dominated by the Montserrat mountain range, known for its distinctive 'sawed' appearance. You can expect rounded, steep peaks, deep gorges, and unique rock formations. Trails often feature significant elevation changes and can range from well-trodden paths to more challenging, rocky ascents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Marganell?
A: While many trails in the Montserrat Natural Park are challenging, there is at least 1 easy route available around Marganell. These easier options are generally more suitable for families, offering a chance to experience the unique landscape without extensive difficulty. Always check the specific trail details for suitability with children.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking?
A: The region offers several significant landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Summit&lt;/a&gt; is the highest point, offering panoramic views. Other notable spots include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/238697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, various hermitages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Onofre Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermits' Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Marganell?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14982344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Summit – Sant Jeroni Summit loop from Monistrol de Montserrat&lt;/a&gt; is a long circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agulles Vicenç Barbé Refuge – Roca Foradada (Holed Rock) loop from Parc Natural de la Muntanya de Montserrat&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a demanding circular experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Marganell?
A: The trails around Marganell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic scenery, the unique rock formations, and the rewarding panoramic views from various summits and viewpoints.

Q: Is it possible to reach trailheads by public transport?
A: While Marganell itself is a smaller village, the Montserrat Natural Park, which is the primary hiking area, is accessible by public transport. Options like the rack railway or cable car from Monistrol de Montserrat can take you closer to some trailheads and the monastery area, reducing the need for a car for certain routes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that lead to the Sant Jeroni Summit?
A: Yes, several trails lead to the Sant Jeroni Summit, the highest point of Montserrat. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31973884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Summit – Old Path to Sant Jeroni loop from Viserta&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive experience of the mountain's ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Marganell?
A: The Montserrat region generally offers good hiking conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler temperatures but some higher trails might be more exposed to wind or occasional frost.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Marganell is an excellent base for challenging hikes. The majority of routes (13 out of 20) are classified as difficult. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19194947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel Hermitage loop from Monistrol de Montserrat&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route with significant elevation gain, suitable for experienced trekkers.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the hiking areas?
A: The Montserrat Monastery complex itself has facilities including restaurants and accommodation. Additionally, nearby towns like Monistrol de Montserrat offer various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options for hikers looking to refuel or stay overnight after their adventures in the park.

Q: What are the geological features that make Montserrat unique?
A: Montserrat's unique geology is characterized by its 'sawed' mountain range, with rounded, steep peaks and deep gorges. These formations are a result of intensive weathering of conglomerate rock, creating a dramatic and distinctive landscape that is a highlight for hikers and climbers alike.
tips:
Located in the middle of the Montserrat region of Agulles, this is one of the two preserved facilities that the Federation has in the Montserrat massif. It is named after the climber Vicenç Barbé, who died in 1942 while climbing the Frares Encantats. Inaugurat el gener de 1961, la iniciativa de construir aquest refugi fou de la Delegació Catalana de la Federació Espanyola de Muntanyisme (l’actual FEEC). Hiking, equipped paths and above all climbing are the star activities that can be done from this refuge. Different tours around the Agulles region (through the Estret gate, through the Porc pass); the return to les Agulles, les Frares and Echos through the Migdia gate; the crossing to the Monastery on the GR 172; the PR-C 78 - which passes through the refuge-; and 900 classic and sport climbing routes that climb to the top of 160 rocks and needles are some of the arguments within reach of the mountaineer.

Services at the shelter:
- Wood stove
- Internet (with usage restrictions)
- Blankets
- Food and drinks
- toilet

Material in the absence of custody:
20 places 20 mattresses and wood stove.

Key step that, on the way up, allows us to enter the Agulles region, guarded by the Gran de la Portella rock on the right and the Petita rock on the left.
The stepped channel formed by the erosion allows us to progress without much difficulty, although we will have to use our hands, more for balance than for progression.
Extremely beautiful hiking trails with wonderful views of Montserrat and the surrounding area

One of the most emblematic places in Catalonia is undoubtedly the mountain of Montserrat. There are several interesting routes in the area, including the excursion to the Roca Foradada de Montserrat, a large hole (about 10 meters high by 6 wide) with views that will take your breath away. It is incredible that the wind and water have pierced the Roca Foradada de Montserrat in this way. Take the opportunity to take lots of photos!

faqs.question: How difficult is the Roca Foradada, La Portella – Les Agulles – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult hike, requiring good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. While some sections are straightforward, others involve using your hands for balance or progression. The ascent to Roca Foradada includes a rocky part with a rope to assist hikers, and the terrain can be eroded. It's classified between SAC 2-3 and SAC 4-6, indicating a need for alpine experience for the tougher sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail commonly starts from the Can Maçana area, which has an information center and picnic facilities. This is generally the best place to find parking for the route.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning panoramic views and unique geological formations. Key highlights include the impressive Roca Foradada (Holed Rock), the labyrinthine needle-like rock spires of Les Agulles de Montserrat, and the distinctive La Cadireta formation. You'll also pass the Agulles Vicenç Barbé Refuge and enjoy magnificent views of the Montserrat mountain range, the Bages region, and even the Pyrenees from certain points.

faqs.question: Is the Roca Foradada, La Portella – Les Agulles – Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a natural park, specific regulations for dogs can vary. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in Parc Natural de la Muntanya de Montserrat, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure safety, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for encounters with other hikers or climbers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking this route, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous sections more challenging, while winters can bring colder weather and potentially icy conditions on higher, exposed parts. Mid-July offers a unique opportunity to see the sunrise through Roca Foradada.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail or to access the general hiking areas within the Parc Natural de la Muntanya de Montserrat.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when hiking in Parc Natural de la Muntanya de Montserrat?
faqs.answer: As this route is located within the Parc Natural de la Muntanya de Montserrat, remember to stay on marked paths, do not disturb wildlife or vegetation, and carry out all your trash. Open fires are strictly prohibited, and camping is generally restricted to designated areas. Always respect the natural environment and other visitors.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult terrain, sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential. Wear comfortable, layered clothing suitable for varying weather conditions. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can be helpful for stability on uneven sections.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other well-known paths. You'll find it follows sections of the Sender del Mil·lenari de Montserrat, E09, and El Camí IT6TR7 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, among others. It also shares parts with the Travessa d'Agulles i Frares Encantats and the Camí de la Roca Foradada.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities nearby the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The common starting point at Can Maçana typically offers an information center and picnic facilities. For more extensive amenities like cafes or accommodation, you would need to visit nearby towns or the main Montserrat monastery area, which is a short drive away.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, characterized by narrow, stony paths and the distinctive conglomerate rock formations of Montserrat. You'll navigate through areas with holm oaks, box trees, and white pines. Expect eroded sections, especially on descents, and be prepared for parts where you might need to use your hands for balance, particularly around the La Portella Pass and the ascent to Roca Foradada.

waypoints.name: View of Les Agulles de Montserrat
